evidence: The amber probe is the single attention-colored manipulator while the periwinkle candidates and lilac bars read as supporting apparatus of one similarity meter, giving a clear hierarchy.
Only three concept types are present — the probe, the repeated candidate arrows, and their repeated score bars — not five-plus distinct ideas.
Each candidate carries a c1..c4 label at its tip, the probe is labeled on its object, and every bar is labeled beneath it with no legend or key.
The only prose is a short instructional caption 'Drag the probe to score each candidate. Drag c1's handle to change its length,' which annotates the visible interactive rather than replacing it.
The candidate labels, instruction, and mono score readouts (0, 1, -1) are all legible across the 1280 and 375 frames despite the digits being intentionally quiet.
All arrows, labels, bars, and readouts sit well inside the frame with generous margins in every state including the 375 width.
Stacked labels like 'c1' and 'probe' in the aligned frame sit on separate lines without overlapping, and no label is stamped across an unrelated line.
The probe is amber (attention), candidates periwinkle (observed), score bars lilac (parameters), and labels cream, each held consistently across all states.
Every element — probe, candidates, length handle, bars, readouts, drop-lines — carries propositional content with no decorative filler.
Each driven state delivers its promise: aligned peaks c1, orthogonal peaks c3, anti-aligned drops c1/c2 below the baseline as negative, and length-cheat lifts c1's score at a fixed 90-degree probe.

The score bars are ordered c1..c4 left-to-right along the bottom while the candidate arrows run c4..c1 left-to-right in space, so each bar sits under the wrong candidate and the drop-lines do not align with the bars.
suggestion: Reorder the bars to match the candidates' angular left-to-right order (c4,c3,c2,c1) and align each bar directly beneath its candidate's tip drop-line.

evidence: The driven states show the same diagram at successive probe angles, reading as a continuous rotation rather than unrelated cuts.
The composition is centered with ample margins and a clear top-to-bottom reading order from instruction to diagram to score bars.
